item.description: A new rule gives cops warrantless access to phones and emails. Foreign businesses, beware.

item.title: Maduro’s Lawyer Says He Won’t Get Fair Trial Without Money to Pay Legal Bills
item.description: The ousted Venezuelan president’s lawyer said the U.S. government has barred his country from contributing.

item.title: Inside the Trump-Spain Rift Testing the Trans-Atlantic Alliance
item.description: In an interview with WSJ’s Gordon Fairclough, Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said that alignment with the U.S. over Iran is a mistake, arguing for a more autonomous and competitive Europe.

item.title: Bank of Canada to Protect Economy From Risk of Persistent Inflation, No. 2 Official Says
item.description: Officials expect the recent increase in energy prices stemming from the Iran war to lead to higher inflation in the coming months, Senior Deputy Gov. Carolyn Rogers said.

item.title: Opinion | My Book Is Banned in Hong Kong
item.description: A store owner and three employees were arrested over a Jimmy Lai biography.

item.title: Israel Races to Pound Iran’s Arms Production Before War Ends
item.description: Targeting priorities have shifted to inflicting lasting damage to industrial sites, as hopes of regime change fade.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Halts Yearslong Effort to Bring Skiing to the Desert
item.description: The $38 billion Trojena project, the first outdoor ski resort in the region, was planned as a geography-defying statement of Saudi Arabian ambitions.

item.title: Air Canada CEO Issues Apology Over English-Only Message
item.description: Air Canada Chief Executive Michael Rousseau issued an apology about his inability to speak French after facing sharp criticism from Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney.

item.title: Arms Maker CSG Eyes Deals in Push to Boost Production, CFO Says
item.description: The company expects to grow production of medium and large caliber ammunition—its main revenue driver—through acquisition and joint venture deals with suppliers, Zdenek Jurak said.

item.title: EU Warns Porn Sites Over Access by Minors, Probes Snapchat
item.description: The EU launched a probe into Snapchat and warned Pornhub, Stripchat, XNXX and XVideos they weren’t doing enough to prevent minors from accessing adult content.

item.title: EU Lawmakers Vote to Advance U.S. Trade Deal
item.description: The move brings the accord one step closer, paving the way for negotiations between lawmakers, EU countries and the European Commission.

item.title: Growth in Asia Could Slow Sharply on Prolonged Mideast Conflict, ADB Says
item.description: A prolonged Middle East conflict could cut economic growth in developing Asia and the Pacific by up to 1.3 percentage points through 2027.

item.title: Carbon-Removal Credits Licensed by EU Draw Interest From Investors
item.description: European Union framework aims to spur further investment in initiative potentially worth hundreds of billions of dollars.

item.title: Thyssenkrupp Warns 1,200 Steel Manufacturing Jobs at Risk in Germany, France
item.description: Its Isbergues site in France would now be closed from June to September, and the steel division production in Gelsenkirchen, Germany had already been temporarily shut down, it said.

item.title: Stock Futures Slip and Oil Climbs Back to $100
item.description: Stock futures moved lwoer as efforts to end the war in Iran showed few signs of meaningful progress.

item.title: BOE Must Be Patient in Considering Response to Middle East War, Says Breeden
item.description: There was a possibility that inflation could fall below target over coming years if there was a significant impact on economic growth, the Deputy Governor said.

item.title: Iran War’s Impact on European Financial System ‘Contained’ for Now, ECB’s De Guindos Says
item.description: De Guindos said the war had had a modest impact on Europe’s financial system, but could yet lead to stress.

item.title: European Gas Prices Rise as U.S.-Iran Talks Uncertainty Fuels Supply Concerns
item.description: European natural-gas prices rose, with the benchmark Dutch TTF front-month contract up 2.7% to 54.23 euros a megawatt-hour.

item.title: Iran Is Applying the Lessons of Russia’s Drone Warfare Against America
item.description: Technologies that the U.S. military is only beginning to adopt could dominate the battlefield if President Trump orders a ground operation.

item.title: As Bombs Rain Down, U.S. Firms Press Ahead With Gulf Expansion
item.description: The Iran conflict adds risks to megaprojects by Western companies looking to tap the Middle East for much more than oil.

item.title: Trump’s Ambivalence on Taiwan Opens a Historic Opportunity for China
item.description: Deterring an attack on the island has been a bedrock American policy for 75 years. Xi Jinping sees a chance to change that.

item.title: Trump Tells Aides He Wants Speedy End to Iran War
item.description: The president has said that he wants to wrap up the conflict in the coming weeks.
